content_of_post_to_comment: It was a Saturday morning and my boyfriend was yelling at me because I was seeing friends that night. We were at my apartment. My windows were open because it was still Autumn and we had spent the previous night at a Halloween party with friends. I knew my neighbour would be able to hear the argument. I was yelling back. I broke up with him two weeks later on a train back from a weekend away with friends. I sent him a text to end it. I told him to leave my spare key in a lockbox. I blocked his contact and haven’t spoken to him since. I had the freedom to end that relationship. I was living in an apartment I was paying the rent for. I had a job. I had an education. Women before me had paved the way to my escape. They had paved my way to tell him ‘I don’t want this anymore.’ And I am forever grateful for that.
